parts.answer: An explanation that includes three of the following:  nitrogen used to form chlorophyll (1)  to form {amino acids/ proteins} (1)  (for) enzymes (1)  to form {nucleotides / RNA / DNA/ribosomes} (for the synthesis of chromosomes) (1)  to produce a named {protein/ organelle/membrane} involved in cell division (1) Accept relevant named enzyme accept ATP/other organic compounds that can be formed e.g. tubulin/spindle fibres (3) (Total for Question 1 = 9 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

Emphasis is on risk to humans  suitable risk identified (1)  suitable control of risk (1) e.g. harmful bacteria /infection/pathogens/biohazard not contamination unqualified e.g. good aseptic technique /specific example e.g. limited lifting of lid/gloves/incubate below body temperature ignore allergies (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

Suitable working shown  correct of log 10 values (1)  correct of denominator (1)  correct answer to three sig figs (1) ECF for MP2 and 3 4.15 & 2.95 0.602 (allow 0.301x2) 1.99 / 1.98 Correct answer (1.99/1.98) without working 3 marks Allow one mark only either for log 14000 – log divided by 0.301x2 OR just 0.301x2 Allow 2 marks for a correct answer with the wrong sig figs and no working e.g. answer 1.986 (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark Any two from Abiotic  temperature  pH  oxygen  growth medium Mark the first answer on each line Unless the second answer on a line is a biotic factor (negates) (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  variable with suitable control method described;  results are not valid / description of expected effect on the dependent variable; An inappropriate biotic/abiotic variable can still gain both marks E.g. incubator/AC/Thermostatically controlled waterbath Accept reduced validity (2) (Total for Question 2 = 9 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

There is no (significant) difference between the (mean) time before extension in mud and sand (habitat) Accept equivalent correct answers Eg time to re-emerge/ different substrates (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  suitable table with correct raw data (1)  with headings with units (1)  correct means (1) MP1 do not accept an extra column Time before extension /s Mud (habitat) Sand (habitat) 14.8 14.0 8.2 7.4 9.9 9.2 11.6 12.6 12.0 10.7 14.2 7.8 10.7 8.0 11.2 11.1 8.9 10.7 12.1 10.8 12.9 11.2 Mean 11.5 Mean 10.3 Means must be to the same number of decimal places (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  axes labelled including units, and linear scale starting at zero without a break (1)  data plotted correctly in bar graph format (1)  range bars correctly plotted (1) Mean time (for extension) /s mud sand Ignore line if drawn Mean 11.5 10.3 Range mud 8.2 -14.8 sand 7.4 - 14 (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  correct numerator (1)  correct denominator (1)  correct answer (1) Allow ECF if incorrect mean values used for MP2 and 3 1.2/1.182 0.763 /0.873 1.37 /1.35 Correct answer gains 3 marks Correct substitution into formula gains one mark instead of mps 1 and 2 (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  the critical value is 2.09 (1)  calculated value is less than the critical value, therefore accept the null hypothesis (1)  there is no (significant) difference between the extension time for mud and sand animals (1)  comment on variability of data (1) Accept ECF for calculated values greater than 2.09 Followed by logical statements Accept there is a difference if they have rejected null hypothesis Eg range bars overlap (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  opsin changes when stimulated by {light/photon} / changes retinal from cis to trans / a different isomer (1)  causes an {action potential/impulse} (in sensory) nerve (1)  causing a reflex response (1) Ignore refs to rhodopsin Accept descriptions leading to hyperpolarisation A

An answer that includes eight of the following:  clear statement of the dependent variable (1)  method of producing standardised sample sites (of known area on both sites) (1)  standardised {method/time} of counting on both sites (1)  identify two variables to be monitored (1)  Describe how one variable can be {monitored/controlled} (1)  repeats for {one/either} sample area (1)  repeat the whole investigation at different times of year (between November and March) (1)  method of calculation of population density (1) Eg number of grasshoppers per unit area Allow use of nets/quadrats/camera suitable way to mark/recapture grasshoppers /Lincoln index Accept control Eg temperature, light(intensity) , humidity, rainfall, pH, time of day, weather use similar aspect/slope of road and gravel area Accept different days (8) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark with means calculated from repeats (1)  bar graph with labelled axes (1)  use of an appropriate statistical test for difference (1) Accept any headers with units Accept description of calculating mean Do not accept units in the body of the table Accept labels on graph from table (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark  difficulty in identifying this species (1)  difficulty in seeing/making grasshopper move. (1)  difficult to ensure each grasshopper counted is a new individual (1)  difficult to {measure/monitor} a named variable (1)  Sampled once so population may change over time Accept rare/hard to find Accept hard to catch them Accept cannot/difficult to control Accept can only be sampled once as they are endangered (3) (Total for question 4 = 17 mark Pearson Education Limited.
